questions.q: On which date did India become independent?
questions.explanation: India became independent at midnight on 15 August 1947.

questions.q: Who delivered the famous 'Tryst with Destiny' speech on the eve of independence?
questions.explanation: Jawaharlal Nehru gave the 'Tryst with Destiny' speech on the night of 14-15 August 1947.

questions.q: At what time was India's independence proclaimed?
questions.explanation: Independence was proclaimed at the stroke of midnight between 14 and 15 August 1947.

questions.q: What was raised as British rule formally came to an end?
questions.explanation: The national flag was raised as British rule formally ended.

questions.q: Who became independent India's first Prime Minister?
questions.explanation: Jawaharlal Nehru took office as the first Prime Minister of independent India.

questions.q: India gained freedom from the rule of which country?
questions.explanation: India became independent from British colonial rule in 1947.

questions.q: The phrase 'Tryst with Destiny' is the title associated with which event?
questions.explanation: 'Tryst with Destiny' is Nehru's celebrated speech marking India's independence.

questions.q: Independence Day is celebrated in India every year on which date?
questions.explanation: 15 August is observed annually as Independence Day in India.

questions.q: The 'Tryst with Destiny' speech was delivered in which assembly?
questions.explanation: Nehru addressed the Constituent Assembly in New Delhi on the night of independence.

questions.q: In which year did India achieve independence?
questions.explanation: India achieved independence in the year 1947.

questions.q: Who was sworn in as independent India's first Governor-General on 15 August 1947?
questions.explanation: Lord Mountbatten became the first Governor-General of the new Dominion of India.

questions.q: From which historic monument did the Prime Minister address the nation on the first Independence Day?
questions.explanation: Nehru hoisted the flag and addressed the nation from the ramparts of the Red Fort in Delhi.

questions.q: Independence came at midnight partly because some astrologers considered 15 August an inauspicious date by day; which compromise was reached?
options: The transfer was timed for midnight
The date was changed to September
It was held at noon instead
questions.explanation: To reconcile concerns, the transfer of power was set for the auspicious midnight hour rather than daytime.

questions.q: Where was Mahatma Gandhi on the day of independence, 15 August 1947?
options: In Calcutta working to quell communal violence
questions.explanation: Gandhi was in Calcutta, fasting and working to calm communal violence, rather than joining the festivities in Delhi.

questions.q: The flag raised on the first Independence Day featured which central emblem?
questions.explanation: The adopted tricolour bore the Ashoka Chakra at its centre, replacing the earlier charkha design.

questions.q: Independence was accompanied by which painful event in 1947?
questions.explanation: Independence came alongside the partition of British India into India and Pakistan.

questions.q: Which neighbouring nation was created the day before India's independence?
questions.explanation: Pakistan came into being on 14 August 1947, a day before India's independence on 15 August.

questions.q: Who presided over the Constituent Assembly session on the night of independence?
questions.explanation: Rajendra Prasad, President of the Constituent Assembly, presided over the historic midnight session.

questions.q: Nehru's speech declared that India would 'awake to life and freedom' while the world was doing what?
questions.explanation: Nehru said that at the midnight hour, when the world slept, India would awake to life and freedom.

questions.q: The national song or anthem associated with the independence celebrations and adopted later as the anthem was which?
questions.explanation: 'Jana Gana Mana', composed by Rabindranath Tagore, was later adopted as India's national anthem.

questions.q: Who served as independent India's first Deputy Prime Minister from 1947?
questions.explanation: Sardar Patel became the first Deputy Prime Minister and Home Minister of independent India.

questions.q: The legal basis for independence on 15 August 1947 was provided by which act?
questions.explanation: The Indian Independence Act 1947, passed by the British Parliament, gave legal effect to the transfer of power.

questions.q: Which British statesman, as Prime Minister, presided over the granting of Indian independence in 1947?
questions.explanation: Clement Attlee was the British Prime Minister whose government granted India independence in 1947.

questions.q: Sarvepalli Radhakrishnan made a notable speech in the Constituent Assembly just before Nehru's on the night of independence; what role did he later hold?
options: Vice-President and then President of India
questions.explanation: Radhakrishnan, who spoke that night, went on to become India's Vice-President and later its second President.

questions.q: The conch shell and ceremonial moment in the Assembly were followed by members taking what kind of pledge?
options: A pledge of dedication to the service of India
A pledge of allegiance to Britain
questions.explanation: Assembly members took a solemn pledge of dedication to the service of India and her people at the midnight session.

questions.q: Why was Gandhi notably absent from the official independence celebrations in Delhi?
options: He was distressed by partition and worked among riot-hit communities
questions.explanation: Grieved by partition's bloodshed, Gandhi stayed in Calcutta to promote communal harmony instead of celebrating.

questions.q: What principle did the composition of Nehru's first cabinet in 1947 reflect?
questions.explanation: Nehru's first cabinet was deliberately broad and representative, including figures from varied backgrounds and communities such as Ambedkar and Shyama Prasad Mookerjee.

questions.q: The lowering of the Union Jack at independence was handled sensitively; what gesture marked the change of flags?
options: The Union Jack was burned publicly
The Union Jack was not pulled down but the tricolour was raised alongside
questions.explanation: To avoid humiliating Britain, the Union Jack was simply not raised on 15 August while the tricolour went up in its place.

questions.q: Independence Day 1947 fell on which day of the week?
questions.explanation: 15 August 1947 fell on a Friday.

questions.q: The midnight session at which Nehru spoke was held in which building in New Delhi?
options: The Constituent Assembly Hall (now part of Parliament)
questions.explanation: The historic midnight session convened in the Constituent Assembly Hall, located within the Parliament House complex in New Delhi.
